page: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The architecture is arm64, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 945 GB of local storage. The instance is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 307.54 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 124.58 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 33.08 GB/sec. Conversely, CPU-bound tasks like stress-ng single-core and multi-core performance fall into the average range. For language model tasks, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 263.31 tokens/sec on small 135M models, though prompt processing on medium 7B models is weaker at 24.62 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16plds_v6 offers a resource-dense ARM architecture with dedicated physical cores and bundled local storage, making it a viable option for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and localized disk access without requiring massive RAM footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-focused tasks, web serving, Redis key-value stores, and software compilation. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpldsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM and 945 GB of local storage. Operating on the arm64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 307.54 GB/sec and writes at 124.58 GB/sec. While its CPU-bound stress-ng and Geekbench scores sit in the average tier, it exhibits strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and small-model text generation. Given its dedicated core allocation and integrated local disk, the D16plds_v6 is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations where high memory bandwidth is prioritized over large memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, optimized for compute-focused workloads requiring high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, managed via Hyper-V.
tagline: An ARM-powered compute instance featuring sixteen dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and integrated local storage for efficient parallel processing.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory and 945 GB of bundled local storage
Strong memory bandwidth with 307.54 GB/sec uncached large-block reads
Average CPU performance across stress-ng and Geekbench suites
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and Redis deployments
